entities.name: CABBAGE LOOPER
entities.description: Trichoplusia ni, the insect whose feeding preference and food utilization were studied

entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: Glycine max, including insect-resistant PI 227687 and insect-susceptible Davis varieties

entities.name: LIMA BEAN
entities.description: Phaseolus lunatus, specifically Henderson's Bush variety

entities.name: FEEDING PREFERENCE
entities.description: The preference of the cabbage looper among different legume species

entities.name: FOOD INTAKE
entities.description: The amount of food consumed by the cabbage looper

entities.name: FOOD UTILIZATION
entities.description: The efficiency with which the cabbage looper uses food for growth

entities.name: EFFICIENCY OF CONVERSION OF DIGESTED FOOD
entities.description: ECD, the efficiency of converting digested food into body matter

entities.name: GROWTH RATE
entities.description: GR, the rate of larval growth

entities.name: CONSUMPTION INDEX
entities.description: CI, a measure used to indicate antibiotic effects of secondary substances

entities.name: ANTIBIOTIC EFFECTS
entities.description: Effects of secondary substances that inhibit food utilization

entities.name: HOST-PLANT SUITABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of a plant species to support the growth and development of the insect

entities.name: SECONDARY SUBSTANCES
entities.description: Chemical compounds in plants that can have phagoexcitant or antibiotic effects

entities.name: LABORATORY CONDITIONS
entities.description: The controlled environment in which the feeding studies were performed

relationships.source: EFFICIENCY OF CONVERSION OF DIGESTED FOOD
relationships.description: positively associated on lima bean and negatively associated on PI 227687 soybean
